headline: It shouldn't take 30 minutes to buy 1 item.
review_content: I went to walmart this afternoon to get a large item at the Portland Oregon Jansen Beach location.  They had the item I was looking for but the service was terrible!  I'm disabled and I needed help getting the item in my cart, the 4 different call boxes I addressed  to get assistance didn't work, and there was no one in the electronics area working to help me lift the box into my cart.  A younger woman who saw me helped me after about 15 minutes trying to locate an employee for help.  When I went to the checkout, there was only one checker plus the self checkout.  Self checkout was open and there was a huge full cart in the checker line with about 50 items, I chose the self checkout.  My item was a Barbie house ($180.00), the bar code was not found said the register, and there was no employees anywhere to be seen except a checker on the one register.  At that point I decided to get in the line with the checker.  The checker was moving along awesomely considering the cart was over loaded with many small items, dishes and specity items that needed to be wrapped in paper, etc..  The checkout was frustrating especially when I noticed 3 employees laughing and huddled in conversation, as then I realized one of the employees was not attending to the self checkout area.  This is the reason why I couldn't find help in the self checkout, he was visiting with 2 women employees.  By then the self checkout had a long line and I was so surprised that a second checkout line wasn't opened.  Honestly I buy most everything online from walmart, and this is not my regular walmart store.  I'm happy that I was able to find the item I needed, but my experience to run in and out to get 1 item took about 30 minutes. Quite frustrating and understandable in most situations lacking staff I understand, but I won't be returning to this particular store since the employees seemed to be more interested in visiting than taking care of their job responsibilities.

headline: walmart has STUPID and OVERLY RESTRICTIVE return policies on some items! Beware if you buy from them, especially online!
review_content: I bought a new, all-digital, PlayStation 5 from walmart.com around black Friday. After getting it (which by the way, it came in nothing other than the product box, inside of a fairly large shipping box, zero packing paper/ packing peanuts, bubble wrap etc, so plenty of space for it to bounce around and potentially have gotten damaged in transit), over the next few weeks, this being the first PlayStation product I've had in a long time, it wasn't until after getting it and trying to buy a digital game to download onto my all-digital console, that I then found out that all PS5 digital games can ONLY be bought from the PlayStation store, where they are often much more expensive than the disc version of the same exact game. Because of this, I reached out recently on like 12/26 or 12/27 to talk with walmart customer support via chat support. I reached out because I wanted to exchange the product for the disc version of the PS5 and just pay the difference as the disc version is a little more expensive, or just do a return/ refund altogether. I found out during the chat, that apparently, this particular product can ONLY be returned in person, that there is no mail-in return option, which I didn't know but the chat support rep said that it says it on the product page and it's on their "return policy". I explained that I didn't know/see that, and I DON'T DRIVE, and that the nearest walmart store to where I live is about an hour and a half away, at least, and I HAVE NO WAY TO GET THERE to return it in person. Anyway, they still gave me the customer support phone number and suggested it would still be worth calling as maybe they could do something since they can "do more" than chat support can. So I called phone support on (Sat. 12/28/24) and spoke with someone named Geo, who I explained and referenced that chat convo to, she reiterated what the chat person said and there was nothing she could do, so she said she was going to escalate me to their "Senior Specialist", once I got him, "Benjamin", he was zero help, sounded like he really didn't care (going "mmmhhmm" at multiple points type stuff - he couldn't care less), and reiterated that its "policy" and the only way to exchange or return this product would be in person to a walmart store. I filed a Better Business Bureau complaint against them where they also referenced their "policy" again, and said that they "consider the matter closed".

The customer service rep told me to call back in 2 days with my reference number and the rep would make it right. I was there 45 minutes just to pick up an order. I asked for a manager. A dept. manager came; not the manager of this dept. Another dept. manager came. Then an assistant manager. The manager refused to come to speak with me. The store was not full considering it is one week before Christmas. There was merchandise on the floor in the women's clothing areas, the floors were actually dirty, associates avoid eye contact to discourage customers from asking for assistance. I left my phone number and asked that the manager contact me. It has been 7 hours since I left the message. I do not expect a call. She was too cowardly or apathetic to speak with a concerned customer. These are the reasons that most customers prefer shopping the largest online retailer. This may be just another store to shutter in Beaumont, not from low demand but because of extremely poor customer service and the ineffective and inefficient management.

He told me we were kicked out of guilds because of our awful behavior.  I told him my grandparents fled pre WWII Nazi Germany. headline: walmart run-arounnd
review_content: On Nov. 2 I purchased a few items at my local walmart. When I arrived home I found that two (identical) items were missing. I immediately called the store. It took 15 minutes to get someone to answer the phone. I was eventually connected to a manager and explained to him what happened and that there was no way I could get back to the store that day or in the next few days. He said that he could not refund my card but that if I called the 800 number he gave me, they would do that. I called the number and spoke to someone who said that I would receive a refund to my card in 3-4 business days. (First she suggested that I take the items - which I didn't have - back to the store.) After 4 business days I checked my credit card and there was no credit posted. However, I had received a walmart e-gift card for the exact amount of the missing items (minus the tax I had paid). So I called the 800 number again. This time they said that they can only issue e-cards, not refunds, and that if I wanted a refund I needed to go back to my walmart store. At no time during the first call did anyone mention anything about receiving a gift card. I asked them if they could cancel the gift card and do a refund instead, and they insisted that only the store could do that. So this morning I went back to walmart, receipt in-hand, and explained the whole story to the manager on duty, who told me she could not do anything for me, and that I hadn't spoken to the correct people to fix this, but that she would give me the correct number to call and they would handle it. She gave me the same 800 number that I had already called twice. When I pointed this out, she still insisted I needed to call them and that she couldn't help me. I called the 800 number immediately (from the parking lot) only to be told that there was nothing she could do and that if I just went back in the store and explained the situation to the manager, they would fix it.  Wow.  I expected better of walmart.  I feel like a ping-pong ball. If they had just explained on the phone that they could only issue a gift card, I would have declined it and eventually gotten back to the store to straighten it out. So I guess I'm stuck with a credit at a store I never want to shop at again.

headline: I’ve had *mixed* experiences
review_content: I have had *mixed* experiences. I prefer to buy items from them at a nearby walmart store, rather than online — to avoid shipping charges receive them faster. But they often advertise brands & products on their phone app that they don’t stock in the nearby walmart store. And when I arrive at the store, to buy the item, they try to convince me to buy a different oroduct or brand — that they have in the store; or they try to get me to travel to a store far from me. They essentially bait & switch. They also often bait-&-switch their in-store products. They *often* don’t show prices for items — especially in the grocery section; ir they mislabel item prices. It happens so often that my impression is that they do it intentionally. And last but not least: their prices have gotten so high — on everything I used to buy there — that these days I often buy the same items for less, sometimes dramatically less, elsewhere.

headline: Well
review_content: Well, wish I had read up reviews before ordering on walmart. But, I was under the impression is was a step above Walmart in terms of sophistication. We don't have a walmart near us so we rarely ever shop one. But, in comparison shopping for an item walmart was cheaper and they allow paypal as a payment option which is the current 5% category for my credit card points. So, with all of that plus a walmart gift card, we were going to save quite a bit of money on a $350 purchase. Got everything done, paid and waited as our order was processing for pickup. Didn't get any updates, so I checked in on the app to see our order was cancelled. What--the reason just said technical issues. I hopped online to check to see if there were details but nope, nothing. Called website CS (who was nice by the way) and she didn't know why it was cancelled. Her suggestion was to try shopping in store. Uhm, well, the reason I didn't was so I could pay with paypal (and get my credit card bonus points). So, now I don't have my item and I guess I have lost my 10% circle bonus discount. Without that, it's not cheaper to buy at walmart so I might as well just go buy it at Walmart. At least I haven't have any issues with online ordering there and it is only 10 miles from me. I was willing to drive the hour to walmart to use my GC and get a discount but I've lost desire to do any business with them now. I will probably just re-gift the giftcard and let someone else hassle with them. I feel duped like maybe they cancelled the order so I couldn't use my discount (I was saving $35 after all). Oh well. At least it didn't COST me anything. If I am paying the same price, I would rather just go to Walmart. After the issue, I did quick search online to find this as well as reddit posts complaining about the SAME online cancelling issues from 4-5 years ago! That's pretty sad a company has not evolved and improved in that length of time.

headline: shipped (Not) this is definitely not…
review_content: shipped (Not) this is definitely not the case! I placed an order for groceries at 3pm through walmart.com at 3pm. I got a notice that it would be arriving between 5 and 6pm that came and went no one arrived. I called walmart Customer Service that is located at the Store I ordered from on Backus Road in Danbury Ct. They stated that they do not know much about the situation and are not too involved in the process. I than reach out to Instacart, they advise me that if I had placed an order directly through them, they could assist but being that I placed it through walmart.com they do not know much. They put me in contact with Corporate for walmart where then a Rep advised that they were running behind and that the order was scheduled to be delivered between 8pm and 9pm that came and went noone again showed up. I called Corporate nymber I was given supposedly a Company called shipped will be delivering I called them they stated they are trying to do their best to assign a shopper no one yet. I stated I placed the order at 3pm and 6 hours later nothing. I received three different delivery times, and all passed to no avail with no shows. Granted I did not see an area on checkout to place a tip and thought it was included in delivery price, however I put monies in an envelope on my door for the delivery person. I wouldn't think they did not pick up a customer's order for that reason but had my doubts now at 8:47pm. So, I asked the rep to state that I left an envelope with tip taped to my door dor the delivery person maybe they will pick up and bring my order now. The envelope was sitting on my door from 3pm until now. This is my first delivery from walmart and my last. I cannot rely upon them, and I am disappointed in the way they do business and their Drivers outlook on getting their Customers orders to them in a timely fashion when promised.

headline: Awful customer service experience
review_content: Wife and I are visiting from out of country and she was excited to visit walmart in Cottage Grove, MN. We wanted to make a purchase with online-only Visa gift cards, so we shopped the store for the items we wanted and put them in our basket and in our online cart simultaneously. We went to customer service with the items and explained that we have these giftcards that only work online, so we got the items to make it easier to pick when we check out online. She said we need to wait at least two hours to complete the order. I asked why, as I had all the items right here in basket. She said that's how it works as people need time to go get the items. I reiterated that I had all the items right there. She said it doesn't work that way. She called for a shift leader at my polite request. She disappeared to talk to the shift leader over the radio and returned as the shift leader said "there's no way to rush the order," a thing I was not asking for. She said no again and that we can leave the items there. I was obviously frustrated (yet still polite) at what just happened and began to leave, and she smugly grinned and said have a nice day, happy she had "won" against the customer. Really terrible, disappointing experience. 0 stars if I could.

headline: My son bought me a magic chef dryer the…
review_content: My son bought me a magic chef dryer the end of January this year because I was in a very bad relationship that was mentally, physically and emotionally abusive because I was looking and waiting for my way out. My son took it to the storage unit he rented for me and brought me the receipt from walmart because the storage units where my unit was, was being broken into nightly and he knew I would hold on to the receipt. Everything was as good as it could be until the early morning hours of February 27, 2024 when things got ugly and I ended up with 18 stitches above my left eye. I got out of the house and ran down the road when luckily a friend of mine was coming up the road, said she just had a feeling and couldn't sleep until she checked on me. My friend took me to the hospital and got me sewed up then to the court house to get a domestic violence protection order that I got. I finally got an apartment in March and things started falling in place slowly. I finally got the dryer to my apartment and it worked great for the first couple times then it would shut off after five minutes and wouldn't dry clothes. Today I called walmart and they asked for the receipt and some other information that I don't have. I never went back to that house out of fear for my life and I lost everything. List my clothes, shoes, personal items, items that were my deceased mothers. I lost it all !! So walmart sent me an email telling me they are sorry but cannot do anything without my receipt. I think it's crazy asking me to go get the receipt after I said in the email I got 18 stitches above my left eye that night and I fear for my life before they would do anything about a portable dryer I paid $399.00 for. Thanks walmart

I won't be purchasing any more furniture from walmart and I plan to avoid online shopping there altogether. Obviously no one who works there knows what they're doing.

headline: Paying For My Own Promotional Gift Cards!
review_content: I shop walmart online; they have a thing from time to time where if you buy X amount of, say, health and beauty items, you get a $5 promotional gift card for your next purchase. To cut a very long story short, I found out that they have been billing, by separate invoice, my VISA card for each gift card! I've been paying for my own promotional gift cards! So I called and got a guy who spoke accented but perfectly understandable English until he found out why I was calling. First he told me that the charges were pending, and would fall off. Why would they bill my VISA to begin with? But I told him they weren't pending, they went through. Then he started babbling and he was absolutely incoherent. This isn't an attack of, this 'merica, speak English, savagery, it was a dodge. I grew up in an immigrant household-this kind of thing has been going on for generations. It's for self protection-have to wonder what kind of threat this guy was under when confronted with this, but that's just speculation. When I started saying, whoa, he tells me I'm confused. So I said, that's it, we're done, and ended the call. When I got the thing from walmart saying, how was your call, I told them. They asked how I wanted them to respond. I told them. They didn't.

headline: Great Service and Product

The suction power is incredible, effortlessly picking up dirt, pet hair, and debris from both carpets and hard floors, tackling messes quickly and thoroughly, and leaving my home spotless. The cordless design provides unmatched flexibility, allowing me to move from room to room without the hassle of plugging and unplugging, and the battery life is impressive, giving me plenty of time to clean my entire house on a single charge. Dyson's sleek, lightweight design makes it easy to maneuver and store, and the attachments are versatile and simple to swap, making it perfect for cleaning everything from floors to furniture and even tight spaces. Overall, my Dyson vacuum from walmart has been a fantastic investment. It combines powerful performance, convenient features, and stylish design, making cleaning a breeze. Highly recommended!

When I asked him what his name was, he gave me the name martin luther king, which was even more insulting. I was desperate and needed help. This employee, was sarcastic and beyond cruel. I have always bragged about walmart being one of the best retailers out there. However, this experience has made me never want to go back there again. I called walmart Customer service on the 16th to ask why I had to why I had to wait. It wasn't delivered. I was told that sometimes UPS marks it as delivered before they deliver it or drops it at the wrong address and they correct it.  Iasked if she would be reporting it to UPS. She said that I had to call back on April 18th.  I went to do request the refund online today. The site said that if I request a refund they may recharge me later if they don't receive the item back....you know the item I never received. I called customer service again. They did provide the refund (that is why I gave them 2 stars) but told me if I order from them again, and I don't receive a package, I will have to dispute it with my credit card company and contact the police.  I am beyond frustrated. The package showed signed for by some initials I have never heard of. They are treating ME like I have done something shady because UPS obviously misdelivered something. It was a terrible ordeal. Just don't even order anything from them online! I will stick to Walmart and Amazon. They take pictures of the delivered item.

headline: 4 1/2 months to receive a game, and no compensation
review_content: Ordered a video game on Dec 12th, 2023, after finding out that the Xbox One version of this game that walmart sold me doesn't exist.  They waited until a week or two after the promised date to inform me they sold me non-existent goods.  So then I ordered the PS4 version (which this review is about).  They kept pushing the delivery date out until they finally said the item was being packaged for shipping on January 8, 2024.  The process froze right there.  No tracking number, no progress.  I called walmart around 8 times since January 8th (let's forget the other 8-10 calls I made since November when everything all started) and after a ton of run-around and people not knowing what is going on and eventually saying they submitted a ticket that never got looked at, FINALLY a tracking number was generated and they shipped out the item.  I ordered it on December 12th as a replacement for an Order that happened around Thanksgiving (November), which walmart cancelled on me due to their own error.  Received on April 9th of the NEXT YEAR.  This dragged-out 4 1/2 months and wasted at least 12 hours of my time.  2 months in, I had ordered a 2nd copy from Amazon that arrived in 2 days.  Now, I have a 2nd copy of the game and am reselling it.  Also, walmart removed the review I posted for the game, AND have yet to offer me any compensation for my trouble.  I WILL NEVER BUY A GAME ON walmart.COM EVER AGAIN.  Save your time and frustration by getting it on Amazon or Best Buy instead.

headline: If I could give walmart a ZERO rating

I have had to repeatedly reset my VALID  username/password for my online Redcard account every few months for the past several years. Every few months I receive a message that my username/password in incorrect. I re-set my current password again just last month and I received a message that they were incorrect. I frankly had had enough so today called the online customer service phone number and requested to have this issue fixed today, once and for all.  His response: he would open a case file for me and then would help me re-set my password. I hung up. I decided to call walmart's Headquarters phone number to address my complaint. What a joke. There are different phone numbers listed online but they do not connect to walmart's Headquarters. They connect to local department stores. You should know that there is no way to call Headquarters directly. You have to go through guest relations and then leave a review. My last resort was to call my local store and I spoke to a store employee. After I informed her of my reason for calling she said she knew exactly what I was complaining about. "Believe me, I know, I know what you're saying. We here go through it personally as employees over and over again. You're right. We also have to re-set our passwords all the time. I believe it's for security reasons. I know, believe me, it's very frustrating for me too. We were told that after the New Year we would have to re-set our passwords again."

I arrived about 1 45 minutes later and was told my item wasn't ready.   I asked how an item that was already in their store according to the website could not be ready.  They said there was a queue (to which they had no access) and items were only going to ready when the queue said so.  I asked them how long and they said they couldn't give me an exact time.  I said forget it I will just get one off the shelf.  Went back to where they are kept and there were none on the shelf.   Went back to pick up desk and asked for manager.  Got this story about how it would be "unfair" for them to just go get the item for me when there were others in the queue.  Mind you this was 7 PM on a tuesday night.  I was the only one at the pick up desk. But according to the manager, rules are rules.  Finally I got the email and another employee told me there was a bin in the back with my item and they would go get it for me.   Most likely it had been there for a while and the picker just hadn't got to their computer to update.   Clearly the manager at this place has either never heard of customer first policy or is openly contemptuous of it.

headline: All in all, id def order again using walmart online...
review_content: Well this was tough to rate but basically my 1st exp ordering online with shipping was good took longer than they said cupl days to receive. But the 2nd time ordering was kinda bad. It might be related to black Friday being in such close proximity to when I ordered. But my order got split rt away into 2 separate orders. Then the est delivery date just hung there in limbo past the est ship time which forced me to fall since I confirmed the shipper FedEx hasn't actually picked up anything yet. Upon calling walmart the customer service was actually really great. They a real person no less from what I could tell answered rt away & fairly quickly noticed the prob. She placed me in a brief hold & easily refunded the entire order to reorder everything for me which was super cool. She placed my order once again & I was able to see things quickly move thru the norm channels like they were supposed to without getting stuck. She also honored the great prices I got by ordering when I did which I got a cupl then gd at crazy low prices cuz of the blk Friday type sales they were having. They honored all pricing again & my order was received on the est delivery date by FedEx who was also good about keeping their online info about my shipment up to date & accurate a huge plus. It was down to the min updated the whole time & they allowed text or email motivations if u signed up online with the track # so it was excellent that part. The stuff I ordered met expectations but honestly the sizing of things were bigger than I expected. I was gladly surprised considering I got the feeling maybe I was paying slightly more for everything in general had I actually been in store buying it all but this was just the feeling I got (never actually verified this). It just felt like I wasnt getting nearly as much stuff with my amt of cash usually I had that day if I had been in store buying it all but it was just a feeling that's it. Anyway the bath & beauty stuff that did arrive was large size versions of things which that could certainly b the reasoning for the higher price feeling I got at checkout looking back at things now. The shampoo cond facewash & a cupl other beauty bottles I thot I ordered were def value sized options which I didn't know that ordering. Online shopping kinda sucks when it comes to size comparing cuz u really gotta be a numbers person to actually get the real feel on what ur ordering & getting which I'm not at all a numbers chick. I learned that I go by actual visual size shopping for things & use visual comparison when choosing a product to buy in store. So it's blind feeling when online. I usually get shocked by size of a cupl things good & bad but most good when I actually get everything so it's a good not bad thing most orders. The walmart app itself is good & really easy to nav. It could b generally better even suggesting & learning based on my shopping nav history I think. Wish I could turn this off completely honestly. But gats with Amy all that uses the AI learning & based ur history off this in its suggesting going forward. They all pretty much blow in this area impo to b frank. All in all the shipping exp was good & id do it & will probly be an online shopper going frwd after everything. Their customer service exp esp with a bad order that fell thru has to b the reason behind me feeling that comfortable to continue ordering online. So ty for seeing priority in things that matter to me. Cuz I'm very aware of how that order getting stuck could've certainly turned into a huge nightmare using other apps so it's noticeable certainly and worth noting.. product selection was excellent had no prob finding anything. Product reviews are magic & are very similar to how Amazon has grown into that section was beyond helpful & really awesome. It totally lent to which products I actually choose to buy in the end but I even learned a cupl beauty tips from ppl in reading em all during my shopping so that was rad. And companies were generally great at replying to customer questions about their products in that same section. That was equally radAF. Id def order again & will b.

headline: Don't order anything fragile from walmart and expect it to show up in one piece and receive the proper replacement!
review_content: Will not be ordering anything from walmart online again! First, I ordered a package containing a vinyl record and the box was in shambles, plus the record was literally split down the middle. Then, I submitted for a replacement and they sent a CD and the system updated the order for that. Called to say I got the wrong item, and even though I submitted photos, they said their system shows a CD and not the original vinyl I purchased --- EVEN THOUGH I HAVE PHOTO PROOF AND SUBMITTED THAT WITH THE REPLACEMENT REQUEST!!!! So then, I get refunded for the price of the CD which is less than the price of the vinyl. Granted, I bought it with a buy 2 get 1 free special but I would never have bought a cd because of the low value so I lost in my savings that I would've gotten with the Vinyl record. Then they had the audacity to ask if I wanted to purchase the vinyl record from them and I said no way, I'm already out my expected savings and it's only going to show up damaged anyways. I will never purchase from walmart.com again and I certainly wouldn't recommend anybody purchasing anything fragile as it won't be packaged properly and be sent broken, and avoid the nightmare of trying to explain to the help center because they won't take hard evidence over their system's and warehouse faults.

headline: Lost Cell Phone Recovered By Employees
review_content: Tonight I needed to stop at walmart in union NJ to pickup medications which were ready and some household items.  While in the store i utilized my cell phone to make a call and departed the store.  Just to identify the store it is number 1084.  Later realizing that my phone was missing find my phone located it at the walmart store.  I returned to the store and sure enough customer service was holding the phone at the curtesy desk.  This is where the review becomes much more important.  I'm a diabetic and utilize the dexcom system.  All diabetics now how important this system is to watching your glucose levels. Without it your lost with not knowing what your levels are at any given time.  I would like to recognize Jeff, the cart attendant and Lindsay the guest services agent.  Jeff located the phone while returning carts to the store. Lindsay held the phone until I was able to identify it which enabled me to continue to monitor my glucose levels. As I said! its not just a phone ...  THANK YOU!!!!

headline: walmart STEPS ON THE LITTLE GUY
review_content: I have been a walmart RedCard member since October 2021 and always paid my bills on time, in excess of the minimum payment. In February 2023, RedCard returned my 02/23 payment of $26.82 and the reason given was "Payment Reversal - Backdate". There were ample funds so I know it wasn't returned for lack of funds. To this day I do not know why it was returned. The notification was mailed to my husband’s new address, not to me. He is 20 years older than me, and I received it way after. As a result, for six weeks I thought my RedCard account was paid in full. During this time, RedCard Services reported my account delinquent, reduced my credit limit from $500 to $200, and then sent notice to my husband's new address. Based only on walmart's delinquent report, two more creditors reduced my credit limit with them, which in turn reduced my overall debt-to-credit ratio and reduced my credit score by more than 100 points overnight.

headline: Today I came in to get new things to…
review_content: Today I came in to get new things to furnish and move apartments. I’m truly drained and exhausted today. After about 40- 60 mons in the Dekalb Mall walmart I was done. I was ready to check out, and I MEAN CHECK OUT! I was asked to go to register 6 with all the things I needed and wanted for my new space. I was down and not looking forward to the cost of my selections. Down again… At register 6 I was greeted with a smile, charm, and light from Kyle and even joking with me. Then without a pause, Q came in and complimented me on my purchase! WOW?! Am I in a Matrix??? We got ALONG INSTANTLY AND CONSISTENTLY JOKED AND LAUGHED LIKE FRIENDS. Wow! What an experience… I’m going through a very difficult phase right now and even considering damaging myself. Q and Kyle have no understanding of how powerful their kindness, laughter, and personality made my day and created LIGHT in that very short 8min exchange. I’m grateful and thankful for Kyle and Q at register 6, AND the beauty lady at register 8. Thank you. 🙏🏾 I’m grateful.

headline: Awesome customer service experience
review_content: I have read some of the reviews, and all I can say is wow. My review is lot different from most of the others. I live in Memphis, but ordered from the the Horn Lake walmart, because it’s closer to me. On Saturday I placed a pickup order, and since I needed to go, also added some items in my cart. Got notification that order was ready, then drove to store, let them know I was there. Soon afterwards a young woman by the name of Latoya comes up verified that I was the right person then gave me one item. They’re other items on her cart, and I automatically assumed they were mine, but when I told her to put the rest of the order in the back, she said you only have one item. So a little frustrated I said” that can’t be. So Latoya went back into the store to check on my order. When she came back to my car. She asked me pull the order up on my phone, and she showed me where I ordered the wrong item, and also the items that were in my cart were to be shipped to my home on Wednesday 11/8. I apologized to her, and said that I didn’t want the one item because, it was not what I needed. Latoya said no problem, then went back into the store, came back to my car, and not only said the item would be returned, but also gave me a $5 gift card for my inconvenience, although it was my mistake for not paying attention to what I ordered. Now who does that🥰. Reading is essential people. Latoya took my number, called me to assure everything was taken care of. I truly appreciate that great customer service, because I don’t always get it in a lot of places. Thank you walmart for hiring great people like Latoya,😊

headline: walmart/ FedEx are the worst
review_content: My name is Scott, I am a disabled vet. living in Beaumont, TX. I am using my nephew's acct. because I don't have one. I wanted to share my continual negative walmart shopping experiences so fellow shoppers would be aware sense things aren't going to improve. I started shopping @ my local walmart because it was so clean & organized (& because I dislike Walmart). My disability made shopping @ my local walmart prohibited, so I decided no big deal to continue online as I do with Amazon Prime. I had no idea that I would find out how bad customer service would be and what a nightmare shipping to get my orders would be. For some reason reason the majority of my walmart orders are shipped by FedEx (unique arrangement I'm sure). In Beaumont, TX FedEx prefers to deliver to apt. offices rather than the unit, takes less time and they told me that. If the office was closed, they would then mark the item with "delivery address inaccessible." Out of my last 10 walmart 3 recent ones marked delivered never were delivered to me. My last order of 10-19 was marked wasn't. The picture taken to show delivery showed (as always) the bottom 10" of a door, the floor, and a box- could have been anywhere. Called the walmart foreign call center & spoke for 15 mins with a heavily English challenged. I asked for a supervisor. I was connected with an extremely feminine man who spent more than 20 mins making excuses for FedEx (still think he worked for them) and stating that it still might come. We have to wait 2 days. My order before the same thing happened to but I had a different corporate representative that replaced my order using the Shipt service. At the FedEx site I even left additional delivery instructions in capitals- PAST BIG POOL UPSTARES... I contacted my local store and was able to get taken care of, the order replaced. Guess who it is shipped by- FedEx. I am serious folks you can't make this stuff up. I'll pay off my Red Card and go back to Walmart. This is pathetically ridiculous. walmart you should have done a better job.

headline: I’m a long time walmart customer
review_content: I’m a long time walmart customer, but I may have to stop purchasing from them. I realize this may sound like a silly thing, but I’m an older woman and I’m handicapped. They always send everything I order together in a huge box that even the delivery guy has a problem carrying to my door. I struggle with every single delivery just getting it into my house, and then it’s so heavy that I can’t lift it up to a table or chair for opening. I have to lean over to open it and lift the items out individually. It blocks my ability to open my front door until it’s emptied. I have a bad knee and a bad back, and I re-injure myself with each walmart delivery. No other stores have done this to me no matter how large the order. Only walmart. Other stores seem to realize that they may have older customers who aren’t able to lift such heavy boxes and ship in several lighter boxes. walmart has a box you can check if you want them to deliver/ship everything in one package and save $1. I only check the box if I’m not ordering anything heavy.

Look at the quality of their products, the filthy mess of junk around their stores, the longer & longer lines due to no one to run the registers and the incredible increase in their prices. You can say "economy" or "inflation", but Brian Cornell's (CEO) salary hasn't been cut, top peeps in the company are getting fatter claiming hikes are due to "inflation". headline: I went in to buy a chair that was on…
review_content: I went in to buy a chair that was on sale, i found the chair in the baby furniture area.  I went up front & told one of the cashiers & she went to her supervisor to let her know. The supervisor told her for me to wait by the area ( by the chair). On the way another make supervisor ( middle age gentlman w glasses) asked me was i being helped & i told him about the chair also! He told me also, that it wd be just a few minutes for help to assist me in buying the chair. I tnked him & waited . And waited, and waited 40 minutes total & noone came at all!  I was very patient, didnt complain, yell, or do anything because i believed ( stupidly) that i wd get help!  So i in frustration, i saw a younger guy stocking shelves nearby & told him of my problem,he went & got a device to put the floor sample on it & said that it was the last one left, so he helped me wheel it to the customer service  area , it was 45 minutes total by now but i was so happy finally that he helped me!  I spoke  to the nice cashier & she looked it up & found that the sale was off already & it ws the regular price. I tnked her & told her it was way too long for me to find out it was no longer on sale.  I was told i wd have to pay full price for the floor model w stains on it. I declined the chair. I got a comp card but  i dont feel i. Should of had to wait a total of 40 minutes w 2 managers telling me it wd only be a few minutes & also find out it was not on sale anymore!  Leaves a sour taste in future purchases for shopping at walmart!  Cant believe even the managers lie to you & not incontrol of ppl that wd gv me a few minutes to help me even if i found out the chair was not on sale!  No one should hv to wait that long for help!   I was polite all the way to the end but i was very angry at my being treated like this!  Thank  you!  Marni D

headline: There's apparently no such thing as customer service at this establishment
review_content: I recently returned an item to walmart only to find out that the item is a 3rd party vendor item.  walmart provided the return label for UPS but did not state the item had to go to the 3rd party vendor's distribution center instead of walmarts.  Why, I ask, did walmart provide a UPS return label that sent it to Indiana instead of NJ?  In speaking with walmart's "no customer service", they refused to confirm the item had been received even though the UPS tracking proved it had been.  They also could not provide a contact number to the 3rd party vendor in order for me to check to make sure the item was received.  They stated it takes up to 10 days for the item, once received, to be scanned in for proof.  Are these people just lazy or in a union?  Amazon knows your item is coming the minute UPS scans it and they start your refund.  I can't believe this company is still in business with their apathetic customer service employees.  They love the fact they don't actually have to help you resolve your issue and repeat the script about their policy, which is, "There's nothing we can do for you because the item you ordered is a 3rd party vendor."  I actually had one no customer service employee tell me to google the name of the 3rd party vendor in order to contact their distribution center.  I had already done that and there's no way to contact them.  How convenient.  I told the rude walmart no customer service employees they would not get payment from me for the item since I no longer had it.  I'm done with walmart and am tearing up my card.

headline: Horrible management customer service
review_content: Horrible management customer service.   This past year we had twin boys and have spent thousands at walmart as it had become our to go place for anything we needed for our babies.  However this past weekend left a very bad taste.  We went into the store at Memorial attached to Memorial City mall in Houston for what we expected to be a quick hassle free return of a $30 birthday gift our in laws gave our babies.   For some reason the lady at the return counter was having trouble processing the return and in order to avoid holding up the line we decided to move aside and politely ask a lead/manager by the name of Matt Mutschler if he could please assist since we had the item in it's original packaging and online receipt with the bar code that clearly stated "this barcode works as your receipt for in-store returns".  He very rudely and in a condecending manner refused to even look at the item or the receipt and told us that if the system was not finding it there was nothing he could do.  We asked how we could proceed to return the item in this situation since it was purchased 3 days ago and he said he couldn't think of any options and proceeded to walk away.  Without even looking at the clearly new unused item in it's original box and the receipt, he did quickly mention it may be an old item we were trying to return.  Since we had never had this issue before, we decided to call walmart's customer service from the store and they said that what we had is all we needed to process the return.  We approched Matt again while he was speaking with a cashier and addressed this to him.  Since she quickly noticed he had no interest in assisting or even looking at the item or receipt she offered to give it a try herself to see if she could help.  It took her less than a minute to process the return and said she did not understand why the original cashier at the return counter was not able to process and that she might be a new employee.  I understand new employees need time to get acquainted with the system however there is no excuse for a lead supervisor to refuse to even try to assist when one of their new associates is having trouble processing what should have been a simple return.  We hope this gets to someone with some authority at walmart and is addressed and given serious attention since I'm sure we are not the only customers that have been treated in this very unprofessional manner by this supervisor.

headline: I have had an online account for 20…

I went to the store and while at customer service the refund was $3.00 less for the item. I had my iphone with me so the clerk could swipe the bar code from my e mail for the return. When he could not determine why the computer refunded a lower amount I told him I would pull up the order on my phone.  In order to do that I had to sign into my account.  When I did that the order came up and then the walmart site closed and I could not open it again. I never use my phone to order on walmart.  When I got home I found out I was locked out of my walmart account on my desktop. I called walmart to see what the issue was and the rep told me I had tried signing in more than 5 times. I told her that was not the case that I had signed in at walmart and was then locked out and tried once at home. I escalated the issue to a supervisor and they researched it.  They found that because I had used a device that they did not recognize that the security system locked my account. I told them to unlock it but they wanted me to reset my password. I told them I would not reset my password.  If they wanted to keep me as a customer they needed to fix this issue. They told me they would have someone contact me but that was 2 weeks ago. I have sent them an e mail to close my account and do not plan to shop at walmart in the future.

review_content: This will combine two experiences...the first is re: my most recent order on 01/10/23...I put an order in at 2:24am thinking it was going to be a convenience...throughout my"ordering process" I was constantly reminded, that if I order before 3pm same day, I would have 2-day shipping...so I completed the order thinking perfect because I would be available...now I just received a "revised" delivery date w/no explanation...even though my order was placed 12hours prior to the 2-day cut-off time...terrible practice, and now an inconvenience...this is not the only company to display services not rendered, but it is the only one, in my experience, to charge 10ct bag fees...

headline: bad experience
review_content: I made a return on December 27 and we are on January 11 and they only returned the money for one item. headline: Disappointed and Disillusioned
review_content: I have now had two really horrible experiences with trying to order stuff from walmart online. In both cases, the total aggravation and, yes, abuse I experienced at the hands of their customer service reps (one of them kept trying to convince me he wasn't "lying" even though I never accused him of that or even suggested it to him...) who were either overtly aggressive or just totally uninterested in helping me at all. Orders take forever to arrive, are impossible to track and no one to take any kind of responsibility for it. If and when I ever got what I ordered, it turned out to be a piece of crap and not even worth what I paid for it, not to mention the hassle of ordering it. Even my in-person experiences in the stores has been bad - lots of stuff out of stock or in disarray, incompetent staff and again, a lot more money than the product is worth. So disappointed - I used to be a loyal customer, but no more.

headline: Never ever order online at walmart

headline: Very poor policy on purchasing online and trying to receive a replacement…
review_content: Very poor policy on purchasing and replacing a damaged item online! I ordered a crockpot (on sale) to use for Christmas and while it arrived on time (12/24/22), it was broken. I notified walmart that day and was given the option of a refund or a replacement. I opted for a replacement. I was then sent several request for surveys on how the customer service handled this - which I did not answer because it was not a completed transaction. I had not received a tracking number from them on a replacement crockpot being sent even though there was a message in my order account about it, so I finally called them today to ask if they could help track it for me. The agent told that me that since I had already requested a replacement before I could only be refunded my money or could order a new one and then turn around and call them to get the sales price. What??? At this point I was pretty frustrated. When I asked why I had not been sent another one or why another one could still not be sent the agent could not give me an answer!! I did tell him to advise his upper management to come up with some plausible answer for this situation. Ridiculous!

headline: Be careful when doing business with walmart

They make thier own rules in regards to the contracts when you go in to pick up. idc if it was a PS5 or something else. they said in the email there would be no problems with me picking up the order my mom placed for my son. Got there & they refused to let me pick it up. My 78 year old mother had to drive in 30 miles on crappy roads so they could get her id. It clearly stated on the email that she would be notified when it was picked up & I was authorized to pick it up. The manager was so rude actually rolled her eyes at me. My son has autism they got him so upset he started tearing up. Yes horrible customer service & no regards for the customer is always right. How pathetic - walmart you have really disappointed us :/

Experience of going into store to pickup an online order is a nightmare (I was trying to save their staff time). Store staff seem unmanaged and ill-trained. I order online from other big box retailers and the order’s ready in 1-2 hrs if not sooner. For walmart, maybe 4-6 if you’re lucky, alas. No improvement since holiday crunch either….

Then I received 3 other texts that they were picking up my order for delivery. Then a final text that my item was sold at 10am and no longer in stock. I will NEVER shop here online again!

They only thing I was offered was a cancellation of my order. I don't want it cancelled, I wanted the item that was promised prior to Christmas. walmart had no answer to why there wasn't posted anywhere, that their could be shipping delays. AND I can't find the item anywhere now. I expressed my concern to Christopher, that if walmart had said upfront there could be shipping delays, I'd definitely went somewhere else and purchased it in person and not had this entire issue. I don't spend over $500 for one item lightly. It's a big purchase for me. And it means a lot that it won't be under our tree in time for Christmas. walmart offered NOTHING. NOT EVEN A $20 GIFT CARD FOR MY TROUBLE. INCREDIBLY SAD FOR A BIG COMPANY LIKE walmart. I'll never order with walmart like this ever again. headline: Racist at Illinois
review_content: I was shopping at the Illinois branch last Friday when I was approached by a young child with blonde hair. I pinched their cheek and pat them on their head before gasping and clutching my pearls, "What's your name?" the child looked up to me with brown eyes "Luka!" they said, holding a copy of Bayonetta 3 in their hands. I giggled before adding "What a handsome young man you are!" Before I could say "Wash me a dog and slap a hillbillie with a nutcracker" an employee that worked at the store approached me heatedly. Her accent was thick. Maybe Russian. Maybe German. No- she was... Lithuanian. "Hey there!" she called "You swashbuckling n*gger! Don't you dare call my child a man. They're nonbinary" I gasped, unsure of whether I should smack a b*tch or turn the other cheek. But when I looked in her hands I saw that she had a Lithuanian spell potion and would use it if I responded. So I ran out of the store.

headline: 2 hour pickup? No. 2 days & waiting...
review_content: Ordered two needed items online, for store pickup. Everything on site stated 'ready within two hours'. After over two hours with no order update, we travelled to store. Service Desk & Management blamed excessive orders; acted like all customers should magically know the store's order volume at any given time; were not at all willing to work with us. We physically brought my two items (there was only one left of one of the items) to the desk, repeatedly pleading for cooperation so i could leave with my items. They would not allow. Staff was not very understanding, empathetic, polite, nor at all helpful. (One Desk employee was fine, but not at all empowered to address the situation, so we were passed off to others who were not as pleasant). While waiting, another customer was allowed to physically *exchange* an item for a different item -- yet i had previously not been allowed to exchange for the same item. When i had received an item shipped, it was defective; when i brought it in to exchange for the exact item in store, i had to process as a *return* and *new purchase*. This delayed the funds returning to my account; i was charged extra because my order (already paid for) then was not eligible for free shipping. The customer we observed was exchanging for a different size. One of the several excuses we were given for not being able to walk out with the items i had ordered, paid for, and fulfilled, was 'inventory' -- but that apparently was not an issue for the perceived white woman next to us. Despite our offering solutions, suggestions, and compromises to all of their excuses, they would not even consider and were not really listening. "within 2 hours" has turned into two days, with still no merchandise, no refund, no text, no call, no email (all contact information on file with walmart account). Horrible experience, from the store employees and website -- the company site should not claim such order fulfilment timeframe if it is not expected in real life. There were no warnings at all that order volume might be high, nor higher than their capacity to handle. Absurd.

Once I got back to Naples I tried to exchange my item and was told I was a few days too late to exchange. I could not drive back during the hurricane to make the deadline. They refuse to do anything. This has to be the worse customer service I’ve seen. I will never shop from there and hoping my neighbors take that into consideration when thinking of shopping there. So many companies helping us out here and walmart does this. Shame on them!!!

headline: Late/canceled delivery without any notification

I agreed to wait. 3,4 hours later, I still did not receive my order so I contacted them again. Only this time an agent told me there was a mechanical issue and the order was returned to the store. She also told me that she noticed that I contacted them before and the order had already been refunded at that time. That is not true because the refund has just been issued. And I should have gotten a notice about the delay or canceling earlier not waiting until I contact them to tell me that. I asked them to reorder but it is still not a great experience. They will not offer anything unless you seriously ask for it. You might want to think again about the same-day delivery service, especially when you are in a hurry.

I took several pictures, I talk to the store manager (for that day), Who told me that the normal store manager was off today. I called corporate, offered them pictures, they did not seem very interested. I asked for some response back from them, and was assured that my complaints would be taken seriously. I have never been in a store that looked as bad as the store looked today. This should never be allowed to happen when you have people working in a store, and trust me there were many people working in the store. Doing what? I’m not sure. It also looks like UPS tried to tape the box up on the side. It was delivered to me with a wet spot so I knew right away it was messed up. I had to clean other items in the box off & let them dry. Thank God their drain cleaner is not strong because walmart brand Draino had leaked too. All the walmart order packer had to do was secure those 2 items. I know it's super busy but Lord. I sat there hearing that each time I called with my sick son in the backseat crying. The last time I called I asked for corporates number and when I called corporate someone picked up the phone with CHEERING and people CELEBRATING in the background. I did NOT feel heard. I even had to ask if I was on the phone with the corporate office. I cancelled my order before I left because all I needed was MEDICINE for my sick child. I’ll never use this service again and it’ll be a while before I trust walmart to offer good service again. The way I was spoken to by each any every person that was a part of my walmart experience today tells me that walmart will surely be out of business within the coming decade.

headline: walmart mischarges my bank for the SECOND TIME customer service is terrible!
review_content: If I could give them zero stars I would. I buy most of my diapers from walmart because they offer a gift card promotion usually.  This time the deal was but $100 dollars in diapers and receive a $30 gift card. So, I bought two big packs of Pampers and one smaller one for a total of $119.18.  I did drive up pick up and I get to my store to be told sorry they were out of one of the big packs.  No problem, the app told me I could either get it shipped or I could simply go up the road to a different walmart store & pick up the pack they were out of stock of.  I chose to go up the road.  I get to the second location and pull in and then the app shows my order is CANCELLED!  So I call walmart because my bank is being charged the full 119.18.  The first customer service rep was rude and insisted that I was charged the correct amount. I asked to speak to a supervisor and got hung up on.  Three calls later they explained that it’s just an authorization and when the app updates it will adjust for the correct amount and my bank balance will adjust.  Ok, so I wait and 3 days later it’s adjusted on the walmart app for a total of 78.03.  Perfect—until I check the bank and there’s 3 separate and nonsensical charges one for 21.86, one for 33.58 and one for 30.00.  None were for the cost of the diapers purchased, but one was for the cost of the gift card i was promised despite walmart being out of stock by the store manager when I went to the second store. It took two more calls to customer service and talking to a supervisor to get the charges adjusted to reflect the actual cost of what I paid for, a difference of almost $10. They gave me the worst runaround ever, but all I wanted was to be charged for what I actually bought. What a joke walmart customer service is!  Rude, unhelpful and uneducated about their own policies. I never want to be a Karen, but I was so aggravated that I was going to get my just  refund if it meant I needed to call the Attorney General at this point. This is the second time I’ve had an issue with walmart mis-charging my account too.  The receipt has one number and my card was charged a different amount.  The first time it was negligible like 2.86, so I just ate it bc I didn’t want to spend the time, but this time I wasn’t going to let them do this.  I still have not received the money in my bank account and if I don’t get it I will be calling and raising hell again.  It’s such a shame… I usually love walmart but this was a TERRIBLE experience as an amputee mom of 2 babies I’ll NEVER shop at walmart again after this fiasco.  Also, I want to point out that when I ordered on the app it said that there were 8 to 10 of each type of box of diaper in stock, and at both stores their stock count was completely in accurate. Shame on you walmart.  I think I should contact the AG…especially because I can prove they’ve done this before to me.  How many unwitting people just trust that walmart isn’t skimming off the top?  Please check your receipts and debits

headline: So I was the Loveland walmart with 6…
review_content: So I was the Loveland walmart with 6 packages of pens & I had a coupon for each item. The coupons wouldn't scan so I called over the self check cashier to have her manual punch them in. She takes her sweet time looking over the coupons & only allows him to use 4 of them. She tells me that it doesn't go with the product that I'm purchasing.  I ask for a manager & about 3-4 minutes later a so call manager comes up his name is Cisco. He doesn't come to me the customer & ask how he can help he goes to the cashier behind my back & talks as soft as possible. He comes over & looks at the 2 $1.00 coupons ask if I have the product then looks & mess around in my bag & tells me I don't have the products. I told him I did but in a bigger size. He said the coupon is for a 5 pack of pens not an 8 pack. I told him that was poor customer service & he was losing business because of this. I told him to get away from me because he was too close to me a female & I would buy Mt items & ho with my 2 sleepy kids. After he took 2-3steps away he started to talk to the cashier & they both were staring at me which then I told me to walk away because I felt uncomfortable with him. Then he wouldn't leave & I told him that's poor customer service & he said to me that "I could leave if I wanted too". I told him I wasn't raising my voice, not being rude & I didn't curse at them. I told Cisco that I would write a review on this & he didn't care & he didn't apologize for anything. That truly is not how you treat your customers. I was interested same store 4 nights prior asking for the same pens that I just brought tonight & found Cisco asked him if he go in the back to find them. He said he couldn't because he was short staffed & only had 4-6 employees closing that night. He said they were on a pallet somewhere on the floor & he & no one else can help me locate the pens. This guy should not be a manager he doesn't know how to talk & take care of his customers. If it wasn't for us the customers he & his staff wouldn't have a job. This is unacceptable behavior especially for a so call manager!!

I also don't know what kind of labor practices walmart patronizes in terms of manufacturing of walmart affiliated brand items. This review pertains to walmart.com customer service, which in my experience wholeheartedly earns the five stars. Thank you, walmart.

headline: walmart is okay but..
review_content: walmart is okay but... it has room for improvement. The good with walmart is that they have great diaper deals, good beauty capsules, and the once a year 10% giftcard sale. However, it has some drawbacks. First, they charge you more online than it does for in-store - this is why I usually will buy on Walmart that does not upcharge you. Second, their items are usually more expensive than on Walmart and Amazon. Third, their in-store pickup experience isn't as fast as Walmart's and they don't have a call option like Walmart does. For these reasons, I shop on Walmart and Amazon much more than I do with walmart, with the exceptions of several categories that walmart usually beats out in pricing (diapers) or exclusive items (like the beauty capsules). Overall, walmart is still okay and I buy things there (5%), but most of my spending is done with Walmart (75%), then Amazon (20%).

headline: Inactivity Warning Letter
review_content: To: walmart Corporate Headquarters.  I was in the process of making an online walmart order today, but stopped momentarily to check my mailbox for incoming mail. I was a little surprised when I saw one piece of the mail I received was from walmart. It was some kind of "warning letter" stating their policy is to "close" accounts that have been inactive for an "extended period of time". Extended period of time??  What?? Really?? It is now 02/2022. I last used my walmart credit card the end of 07/2021, less then 8 months ago. I appreciated the "warning" but still somewhat confused about the "inactivety" definition. because my last active use. I'm also finding their seems to be a "conflict" with my current walmart credit stating it is good for my use thru "05/2023".  I realize some credit card companies do issue these type of "warning letters", but I've never received one. I have excellent credit scores. I pay all bills fully and on time and feel I am a good customer. It is disappointing that walmart appears to place such little consumer value on good, loyal customers. Before reading the "warning letter" I was already in the process of placing a online walmart order.  The "letter" made me hesitant in completing the order, but I did eventually complete my order inspite of my discomfort.

headline: walmart.com is a wonderful alternative…

They have a decent Grocery area with fresh produce and dairy. At the register, if asked the cashier will price match all of your items. Plus you earn one percent on all of your purchases. Safeway and Foodland don't come close. I recommend shopping at walmart when possible!

headline: Frustration
review_content: First the distribution of your reviews is bimodal.  Love or hate it that should tell you something.  For the last three years I have kept track of what % of my list was filled by walmart.  Normally, just over half.  Today it was 100%, sort of.  My first two items razor blades and floss picks were and easy find.  I needed toilet bowl bleach and after some searching I found it.  I needed shoe laces and a little shoe glue.  No luck.  I talked to and employee and discovered walmart does not keep all of their shoe laces in the same place.  Duh! No shoe glue but the nice employee got a substitute that might work.  For the first time in 3 years hitting 100%.  Maybe the loss of 50% of my business was related to the remodeling where old habits take you to the wrong place.  You know there is a field of study that looks at man and environment interactions it is found in the human engineering section of psychology departments and not in marketing.  walmart it seems to believe if you keep customers moving circular patterns they will buy more with out considering individuals like myself who buy less because everything has been moved into unfamiliar locations.  I think it was WC Fields who described marketing as people you don't know trying to sell you something you don't need with money you don't have.  He was right.

headline: Won't give the money back for items that was not in stock after you payed for them so they cancel that item and keep the money for it!!!
review_content: Never had any problems buying from the actual walmart stores but when it comes to buying from the site walmart.com it is just a horrible experience!!! I ordered off there got the first time today so I could just pick my items up at the store. Well It told me that everything I wanted was in stock which was 2 different flavors of cat food (8 cans in one flavor and 8 in another flavor) so I bought all 16 cans of this. My order total was approved and came to almost 24 bucks. It was immediately took in out of my bank account in seconds after purchasing my order and then a little while later they sent me an email saying that my order was ready but 6 of the cans so about 8 dollars worth of my order was not there. So they had to be cancelled and the had a new price that they said I was charged which was $15.27. they sent me confirmation emails about half the order being cancelled due to being.out of stock but never once said anything about giving me the remaining left over money they charged me. I went to pick up my order and the receipt said I was only charged $14.23 and again I call my bank and they said no walmart took out 23.57, they never gave me any of that money back. And not only that there where 3 different totals that they said I was charged. The first email they sent me before they cancelled half my order said $23.57 which is the amount that was tookin out of my account. Then there was the pickup confirmation which said I was only charged $14.23 (I was never charged that) and then when I clicked on the email it took me to the walmart app page which said I was charged $15.27! So that is 3 different totals for the same order of 10 cans of cat food!! So not only did I get charged for something they dident have it give me they completely lied when they sent me that confirmation saying I was charged 14.23 ot the 15.27!! I was charged 23.57 which would of been the correct total if I would have got my full order but they dident have it all in stock!! I try calling for help with customer service for walmart.com and they refuse to do anything. I have screenshots, bank statements, receipts from ordering this order where it shows how much they took out of my bank account which proofs they took the 23 bucks but they just kept saying we don't charge you any money until after the order is picked up!! Well that's how it should be but that is NOT how it is!!!! I never once got any emails confirmation or nothing stating that they was putting the difference back onto my bank card or nothing!! It just went from an email saying I was charged 23.57 to an email saying some of the order had to be cancelled and your new total charge is 14.23! Never once said anything about the 8 bucks or so that they took from me!! I know it's only 8 bucks but it's the principal! They charged me for something they said they had in stock then after they took the money for something they said they had in stock then they say unfortunately it's not in stock and just keep the money?????? How is that ok?? I will never order from here again this website is completely garbage and I will never let anyone I know order from here!! So if u are someone who orders from here good luck your going to need it!!

headline: walmart's online shopping has been great…
review_content: walmart's online shopping has been great during this pandemic. Several times I've pulled into a curbside spot and the person is standing there waiting for me.  Returns are easy and at times for small items, they've just said "keep or donate" to avoid shipping/hassle.  No company is perfect, but walmart makes things pretty easy.  Online checkout is a breeze and getting easier.  No complaints.

headline: Should be 0 stars; Gift card "department/customer service" is bogus.
review_content: I have had frustrations with walmart gift cards these past 2 years. The first was during their GC promotion last year, so they used that as an excuse due to high volume. This year, I only purchased 2 gift cards without any promotion. One was via mobile, which was supposed to be received within 4 hours. I kept checking the status online and it kept saying it was still preparing. I chatted online with someone who said to come back in 24 hours. I came back the next day and they told me it takes 4 to 24 hours. Well, it was well over 24 hours at that point. The rep told me to call their Gift Card department. There are multiple selections to get you to the correct gift card representative from the GC specific number. They all lead to the same person- a nasty woman that is not in the US and can barely be understood and can hardly speak English! After going through an entire explanation and order/name/address verification, she then says she will transfer you to the correct representative. Wait a minute- I thought she was the correct representative! And to make the issue worse, she pretends to transfer you! You can hear her sitting quietly and waiting for a few minutes. I hung up the first time, called back and it was the same person and the same scenario played out as she pretends to transfer you while sitting quietly on the phone. I had to keep saying, "hello, are you there?" All I wanted to do was find out the status of the mobile GC. She kept insisting that she was going to open a ticket and that's all she could do. I didn't want to have a ticket opened, I wanted to just find out when it was going to be sent! The nasty person gave me a huge attitude and said, "Well it says pending, what do you think pending means?" Really?? It is impossible finding out any info about gift cards as soon as you place the order! I ordered another gift card through the mail and now I wonder if I will even get that. I tried writing a review on their site, and it will only tabulate the rating and not post my review! I will never purchase a GC from walmart again.

headline: This is a 50/50 review
review_content: This is a 50/50 review.  About 6 years ago my wallet with Red debit card in it was stolen.  I went to the store CS desk  to report it and was asked to leave copies of my drivers license and SS card to process a replacement card.  Neither my bank nor credit card company asked for this.  I told the clerk I would show her the ID to prove my identity but I would not allow her to copy or note the info.  I was then refused a replacement card.  I returned to the store another time to try again, same story.  About 2-3 years later I decided maybe I’d have better luck now.  The clerk suggested applying for a new card because maybe the previous account had been closed due to no usage.  Well, again, no luck.  They said my info was already tied to an existing account and refused to replace the card without sending in copies of my ID.  At this point I decided why shop there.  Why would I send in my ID that would be there for any dishonest person to steal?  Fast forward to a few weeks ago.  I became annoyed about not having that damn card that gives me 5% discount.  I decided to apply online for a new account.  Once again, I got a letter that stated my info is tied to an existing account and for help, call.  So I called, skipped my story and just said I was trying to get a replacement of a lost card.  After a short wait, the rep said she had processed the request and I would receive a new card in 7-10 days.  Not in my hands yet, but I’m hopeful.  walmart gets a 0 star for their policy.  But I have a 5 star for the agent who processed my request.

headline: I bought an Amazon Paper White from…
review_content: I bought an Amazon Paper White from this store. It was supposed to be a 10th generation, thats what I asked for and what was on the Box. I also bought a case to go with it. The case, that was recommended by an employee didn't fit I did a lot of research and bought another case. The new case didn't fit either and I called Amazon. After trouble shooting with them for about 30 mins they had me give them the serial number. Turns out the model I had was a 7th generation Paper White. I asked for a 10th generation, I was given a box that said 10th generation but in the box was a 7th generation. Not the end of the world mistakes happen. Then I reached out to walmart and they told me they wouldn't give me the correct device. How a billion dollar company thinks it's ok to sell an older model labeled as a new one is ok is unbelievable to me. I wrote a review on their Facebook page and after pretending they were going to help me out told me there was nothing they could do. Imagine going to buy 2021 Camry and when you get it you realize it's actually a 2015 and the dealership tells you there's nothing they can do. I've been a loyal walmart shopper for as long as I can remember, I go there multiple times a week and probably spend 8-10k dollars there a year. Things go wrong and people make mistakes, I have no problem with that. The fact that the company lied about the product they were selling, disguised it as a newer model and refuse to make it right is very disappointing. To top it off, the company deciding it was ok to not make it right is unforgiveable.

No e-mail sent.  On 07-01-21 I called again to Customer Service to Cancel my order entirely and was told I could not cancel my order because it was not processed yet.  What a Line of Bull.  Wouldn't it make more since to cancel my order before it is process to ship?  I was told that when I receive a message that my order has shipped, then I could cancel my order.  This is a backwards policy.  It cost the company money for labor and material to process an order and get it shipped, when it doesn't cost anything to just cancel the order.

headline: I ordered a small generator for my wife…
review_content: I ordered a small generator for my wife to take camping with her friends, from Amazon and it arrived the next day.  I wanted another one the same to be able to electrically synchronize them for more power when required, so I was going to order another from Amazon, but they were out of that exact unit, and I needed the same exact unit.  So I ordered the same model generator from walmart online, whereby I received a delivery date, which matched when I NEEDED it for this camping trip.  So, I didn't get any  location tracking beyond the first 2 days so I spent time on the phone with a walmart rep who had no location information, indicating it looks to be lost, recommending I cancel that order and they would send a replacement ASAP.  So with so much at stake for my wife's trip, and with guidance from this walmart rep, I cancelled the first one and he scheduled another one to be sent asap.  Again I was given another delivery date, which was actually just the day before they were to leave, but no progress tracking was given once more.  My wife left with just the first one I purchased from Amazon.  I eventually did receive the one from walmart, but my advice is, if you actually NEED something with ANY sort of delivery date, you are kidding yourself if you order online from walmart.  I will never, never make that mistake again.  And really, why buy anything from them, something is very wrong with how they provide delivery dates.

It was a young African American employee bringing the orders to the cars. I was the only car waiting in the parking space and she over looked my  car and serviced the cars that arrived after me. When I saw her helping the other customers I lowered my window to get her attention she said I was yelling across the parking. She then scanned the barcode for the item. The manager Rachel came to my car and I expressed my disappointment about their customer service. Rachel blew me off and said that she would address my concerns with their employee she was rude and had very bad attitude. The service at walmart is horrible.
